next_best_action: Investigate Outlook crash on large inline-content emails. Check for known issues with Microsoft 365 Apps Version 2403 Build 17425.20228 handling large HTML emails with embedded images. Consider Outlook memory limits and the March Windows update as potential factors.
remediation_steps: Check for known issues with Outlook Version 2403 and large HTML emails with embedded images
Review Event Viewer crash data for OUTLOOK.EXE exception code 0xc0000005 to identify the faulting module
Test with a freshly created Outlook profile and a copy of one of the problematic emails
If the issue is related to the March Windows update, check for a cumulative update fix or roll back the update
Consider configuring Outlook to download only email headers for the automated report folder as a workaround
Rebuild the Outlook search index to fix the search issue caused by crash-related corruption

next_best_action: Investigate MFA token expiration preventing client portal access. The base64-encoded error log indicates an expired MFA token. Check Entra ID for MFA registration status and token refresh policy for this user.
remediation_steps: Verify the user's MFA token status in Entra ID and check for token expiration issues
Check Conditional Access policies for any recent changes affecting the client portal
Have the user clear browser cache and re-authenticate to force a new MFA token
If MFA token refresh is failing, re-register the MFA method in Entra ID
Confirm portal access is restored before the user's client meetings

next_best_action: Investigate persistent packet loss on London office Floor 2 LAN. Access switch replacement did not resolve the issue — traceroute data shows losses at hop 3 (core switch 10.44.0.254). Focus investigation on the core switch rather than the access layer.
remediation_steps: Investigate the core switch at 10.44.0.254 for packet loss — review interface error counters, CPU utilization, and spanning-tree topology
Check for duplex mismatches or failing transceivers on the uplink from Floor 2 access switch to the core
Review recent network changes or firmware updates that coincide with the March 11 start date
If core switch is faulty, plan failover to redundant path and schedule replacement
Escalate to vendor support if the core switch diagnostics are inconclusive

next_best_action: SECURITY INCIDENT: Production database credentials, Azure storage account key, Key Vault tenant/client IDs, and a client secret have been exposed in a plaintext support ticket. Immediately rotate all exposed credentials and investigate the extent of potential unauthorized access.
remediation_steps: Immediately rotate the production database password for svc_analytics_ro
Rotate the Azure Storage account key for contosoproddata
Rotate the client secret for app registration a8f3c2e1-4b5d-4f6a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
Review Azure Key Vault access logs for any unauthorized access using the exposed credentials
Audit who has access to this support ticket and restrict visibility
Educate the user on secure credential handling practices — never share credentials in tickets
Implement automated credential scanning in the ticketing system to detect and redact secrets

next_best_action: Investigate SharePoint file upload failures for filenames with non-ASCII characters (accented Latin, CJK, emoji). Check SharePoint Online character restrictions, tenant locale settings, and any recent policy changes affecting filename validation.
remediation_steps: Verify SharePoint Online character restrictions for the Wealth Management site collection
Check tenant-level file naming policies for restrictions on non-ASCII characters
Test file uploads with various character sets to identify which specific characters are blocked
If locale-related, check the SharePoint site's language settings and the user's browser locale
Review recent SharePoint updates or policy changes that may have introduced stricter filename validation
Rebuild or trigger a re-crawl of the search index to fix the missing search results for accented filenames

next_best_action: Investigate VPN failure affecting 47 users in the Singapore office after overnight security update. Critical: MAS regulatory filing deadline tomorrow at 9 AM SGT. Determine if the security update modified GlobalProtect VPN client configuration or certificates.
remediation_steps: Identify which security update was pushed overnight and check for known VPN compatibility issues
Check GlobalProtect gateway configuration and DNS resolution from the Singapore office
If the update broke the VPN client, prepare a rollback package for affected laptops
Establish an emergency alternative remote access method for the compliance team to meet the MAS deadline
Deploy the fix or rollback to all 47 affected users and verify VPN connectivity is restored

next_best_action: Investigate data corruption in legacy CRM to Dynamics 365 migration export. Approximately 30% of 12,000 records have encoding corruption (null bytes, mixed collation, UTF-16 fragments). The legacy SQL Server 2008 source has mixed collation settings contributing to the issue.
remediation_steps: Analyze the source SQL Server 2008 database collation settings across all relevant tables
Build a data cleansing pipeline that handles encoding normalization, null byte removal, and UTF-16 to UTF-8 conversion
Re-export the corrupted records directly from the source database with explicit encoding settings
Validate each record against the Dynamics 365 schema before import
Run a reconciliation report comparing source and destination record counts and data integrity
Document the encoding issues and migration process for the decommissioning audit trail

next_best_action: Resolve persistent HP LaserJet paper jam error on Floor 6 printer (asset tag CT-P-2847). This is the 7th report in 4 days without resolution — previous dispatch and vendor escalation have not been effective. User needs the printer for quarterly compliance reports.
remediation_steps: Dispatch a technician immediately to Floor 6 printer CT-P-2847
Perform hardware diagnostic — check paper path sensors, pickup rollers, and fuser assembly
If the phantom paper jam error persists after clearing, reset the printer firmware
If hardware is faulty, deploy a temporary replacement printer while CT-P-2847 is sent for repair
Ensure the user can print the quarterly compliance reports before the deadline

next_best_action: Investigate report-service OOMKilled crash causing 502 errors on the API gateway. The container exceeded its 4GB memory limit during quarterly report generation. Increase memory limits and optimize the report generation to handle large datasets without OOM.
remediation_steps: Restart the report-service pod immediately to restore report generation capability
Increase the memory limit for the report-service container from 4GB to an appropriate higher value
Investigate the quarterly report generation code for memory leaks or inefficient data loading patterns
Implement streaming or chunked report generation to reduce peak memory usage
Add memory usage monitoring and alerts to catch similar issues before OOM kills

next_best_action: Close — this is a phishing/spam email, not a legitimate support request. The link appears to be a credential harvesting attempt disguised as an Azure certification program. Forward to the security team for investigation.
remediation_steps: Close this ticket — it is spam/phishing, not a support request
Forward the original email to the Security Operations team for analysis and blocklisting
Block the sender domain contoso-partner.biz in the email gateway
Check if any employees clicked the phishing link and initiate credential reset if necessary
